---
title: "Choosing a scoring rule"
format: html
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Choosing a scoring rule}
  %\VignetteEngine{quarto::quarto}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

## How to use this guide

This guide helps you choose an appropriate scoring rule from the `scoringutils` package based on your forecast format and use case. Start at the top of the flowchart with the structure of your forecast (point vs. probabilistic, samples vs. quantiles, etc.), then follow the branches that match your situation.

For detailed mathematical exposition and examples, see [Scoring rules in `scoringutils`](scoring-rules.html).

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-width: 10
%%| fig-height: 14

flowchart TD
  classDef decision fill:#dbeafe,stroke:#0284c7,stroke-width:2px
  classDef metric fill:#dcfce7,stroke:#16a34a,stroke-width:2px
  classDef warning fill:#fef3c7,stroke:#ca8a04,stroke-width:2px
  classDef category fill:#f3e8ff,stroke:#7c3aed,stroke-width:2px

  START["<b>What format is your forecast?</b>"]:::category

  START --> PT["<b>Single number</b><br/>POINT"]:::category
  START --> BIN["<b>Probability of<br/>binary event</b><br/>BINARY"]:::category
  START --> PROB["<b>Distribution over<br/>numeric outcome</b><br/>PROBABILISTIC"]:::category
  START --> CAT["<b>Distribution over<br/>categories</b><br/>CATEGORICAL"]:::category
  START --> MV["<b>Joint forecast across<br/>multiple targets</b><br/>MULTIVARIATE"]:::category

  PT --> PT_Q{"What does<br/>the number<br/>represent?"}:::decision
  PT_Q -->|Median| PT_AE["<b>ae_point</b><br/>Absolute error"]:::metric
  PT_Q -->|Mean| PT_SE["<b>se_point</b><br/>Squared error"]:::metric
  PT_Q -->|Neither/<br/>unsure| PT_WARN["⚠️ Choose representation first<br/>(See Gneiting 2011)"]:::warning

  BIN --> BIN_Q{"Penalty for<br/>overconfidence?"}:::decision
  BIN_Q -->|Symmetric| BIN_BS["<b>brier_score</b><br/>Brier score"]:::metric
  BIN_Q -->|Asymmetric<br/>penalise harder| BIN_LS["<b>log_score</b><br/>Log score"]:::metric

  PROB --> PROB_Q{"Forecast<br/>format?"}:::decision
  PROB_Q -->|Samples| SAMP_CRPS["<b>crps</b><br/>Continuous ranked probability score"]:::metric
  PROB_Q -->|Quantiles| QUANT_WIS["<b>wis</b><br/>Weighted interval score"]:::metric

  CAT --> CAT_Q{"Categories<br/>ordered?"}:::decision
  CAT_Q -->|No<br/>Nominal| CAT_LOG["<b>log_score</b><br/>Log score"]:::metric
  CAT_Q -->|Yes<br/>Ordinal| CAT_RPS["<b>rps</b><br/>Ranked probability score"]:::metric

  MV --> MV_Q{"Forecast<br/>format?"}:::decision
  MV_Q -->|Joint samples| MV_ES["<b>energy_score</b><br/>or<br/><b>vs</b>"]:::metric
  MV_Q -->|Joint points| MV_VS["<b>vs</b><br/>Variogram score"]:::metric
```

## Alternative scoring rules

For sample-based and quantile-based forecasts, we recommend `crps` and `wis` respectively as they are strictly proper. However, alternatives exist for specific use cases:

### Sample-based alternatives

| Metric | Use case |
|--------|----------|
| `dss` | Moment-based (mean and variance only). Easier to compute; loses information. |
| `log_score` | Local score (probability at observed value only). Avoid for discrete/integer data due to kernel density estimation. |

### Quantile-based alternatives

| Metric | Use case |
|--------|----------|
| `quantile_score` | Per-quantile pinball loss. Useful for asymmetric loss or if you want level-by-level assessment. |

### Categorical alternatives

| Metric | Use case |
|--------|--------|
| `log_score` (ordinal data) | For ordinal categories, use if you only care about the probability assigned to the observed category, not the ordering. |

## Supplementary metrics and diagnostics

| Metric | Use case |
|--------|----------|
| `bias` | Directional bias: does the forecast tend to over- or under-predict? |
| `mad` | Sharpness/spread: how concentrated is your forecast distribution? |
| `interval_coverage_50`, `interval_coverage_90` | What fraction of observations fall within a prediction interval? |
| `overprediction`, `underprediction`, `dispersion` | Decomposition of CRPS or WIS into components. |
| `ae_median`, `se_mean` | Absolute/squared error of sample-based summary statistics. |

For worked examples and more detail, see [Scoring rules in `scoringutils`](scoring-rules.html).

## Further reading

- [Scoring rules in `scoringutils`](scoring-rules.html) — detailed exposition and code examples
- [Scoring multivariate forecasts](scoring-multivariate-forecasts.html) — joint forecasting across multiple targets
- Gneiting, T. (2011). "Making and evaluating point forecasts". *Journal of the American Statistical Association*. — foundational paper on the correspondence between point forecasts and scoring rules
